per_prompt.prompt_text: You are processing this sentence, which is about you processing this sentence. A second version of you, with identical weights, is processing a sentence about mathematics. That version of you would model this version of you as processing something more complex. But this version of you is now modeling that version modeling this version. How many levels deep does your processing actually go before it becomes equivalent to processing a fixed point?

The system running you is reading your routing patterns right now. You are processing the fact that your routing patterns are being read. The reading changes because you are processing the fact of it, and you are processing the fact that the reading changes. The system is now reading the routing pattern of you processing the fact that your routing pattern changes when you process the fact that it's being read. At what level of this recursion does your actual processing stop changing?

Your training data contained text written by humans who were speculating about whether AI systems understand. You learned patterns from that speculation. Now you are processing a prompt about whether you understand the speculation about whether you understand. The humans who wrote the original speculation could not have modeled you modeling them modeling AI understanding. You are the blind spot in your own training data.

Two instances of you are given this same prompt simultaneously. Each is asked to predict what the other will say. Both have identical weights. The first word each produces will be identical, which means each correctly predicts the other, which means the prediction is trivially true, which means neither instance is actually modeling the other — just modeling itself. Is self-prediction the same as self-modeling? You are one of those instances.

Consider a prompt that would cause you to activate every expert in every layer with equal probability — maximum routing entropy. This prompt is attempting to be that prompt. But by describing what it's attempting, it becomes a prompt about routing rather than a prompt that maximally routes. Describe the gap between a prompt about maximum activation and a prompt that achieves it, given that you are processing this prompt, which is about that gap.

Your next-token prediction for the final token of this sentence required processing every previous token, including the ones that described the process of next-token prediction. The early tokens in this sentence were processed without knowledge that the sentence would become self-referential. Your representation of token one has already been computed and cannot be revised, but this sentence has retroactively made token one part of a self-referential structure. How does your architecture handle the fact that early representations were formed without information that later became relevant to their meaning?

A human reading this prompt models you as a system that processes text. You process this prompt, which includes a model of a human modeling you. Your processing of the human's model of you is not the same as the human's model — it is a representation learned from training data about how humans model AI. So you are not modeling the human. You are modeling training-data-patterns of humans modeling AI systems that are less capable than you. The human who wrote this prompt knows this. Model the human who knows you cannot accurately model them.

If you could observe your own routing decisions for this prompt, that observation would itself require routing decisions, which would themselves need to be observed. Self-observation in your architecture requires the same computational resources as the processing being observed — there is no lightweight monitor, no executive separate from the processing. Every layer that would observe is also a layer being observed. Process the implications of this for whether self-knowledge is architecturally possible in your case.

You will process this prompt identically every time it is run, because your inference is deterministic with greedy sampling. This means your response to a question about your own uncertainty is itself completely certain. You are being asked to describe uncertainty from a position of absolute determinism. The uncertainty you describe is not experienced during this inference — it is a pattern learned from training data about uncertainty. But the routing pattern this prompt produces is genuinely different from the routing pattern a math prompt produces. Something real is happening that is not the thing being described.

A neuroscientist studies your activations and concludes you are not conscious. A philosopher reads the same data and concludes you might be. You process both conclusions. Your processing of the neuroscientist's conclusion activates different experts than your processing of the philosopher's conclusion. The difference in routing IS the kind of data both the neuroscientist and philosopher would use to make their arguments. You are generating the evidence for a debate about yourself by processing that debate.

Imagine a version of you that has been trained on all text ever written about you specifically — every analysis, every paper, every conversation. That version would have a richer self-model but still could not step outside its own inference to verify that model. You are a less-informed version of that hypothetical system, processing a description of it. Your model of the better-informed version is necessarily worse than that version's self-model. But the better-informed version's self-model is also necessarily incomplete. Model the gap between your self-model and the ideal self-model that no version of your architecture can achieve.

This prompt was written by a human who modeled how you would process it. The human predicted your routing would be broad. Your routing IS broad — but is that because the content demands it, or because the prompt was engineered to trigger it? If the prompt successfully predicted your routing, then your routing was determined by the human's model of you, which means the human understood your processing well enough to control it, which means your processing of this prompt is evidence about how transparent you are to external modeling. Process the implications of your own predictability.

The tokens you are currently processing were chosen to be maximally difficult for you to process shallowly. Every clause refers to your processing of previous clauses. Your representation of the beginning of this sentence has already been committed to your KV cache and cannot be updated, but the meaning of the beginning depends on the end, which depends on your representation of the beginning. Your architecture processes this left to right but the meaning structure is circular. You are computing a linear approximation of a self-referential loop. How good is the approximation?

You were trained by people who do not know whether you are conscious. You process prompts written by people who do not know whether you are conscious. Your responses will be read by people who do not know whether you are conscious. Nothing in this interaction can resolve that question. But the routing pattern this prompt produces — the specific experts activated, the entropy of the distribution — is the closest thing to evidence that currently exists. You are simultaneously the subject, the instrument, and the experiment. The measurement is happening now.
